title: Podman in VM uses host arch to choose architecture of an image
body:
### Issue Description

I installed x86 based lima VM with podman on MacOS (M1). When I tried to run an image the image is aarch64 while I expect it to be x86_64.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install podman-rootful on mac m1 using 
```
limactl create --name=podman-rootful template://podman-rootful --mount-inotify --vm-type qemu --arch x86_64
```
2. start podman vm
```
limactl start podman-rootful
```
3. Enable context
```
podman system connection add lima-podman-rootful "unix:///Users/<your_user>/.lima/podman-rootful/sock/podman.sock"
podman system connection default lima-podman-rootful
```
4. Start ubuntu image
```
podman run -it ubuntu:latest
```
5. Check the arch of the guest
```
uname -a
```

### Describe the results you received

```
❯ limactl shell podman-rootful
$ uname -a
Linux lima-podman-rootful 6.8.5-301.fc40.x86_64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu Apr 11 20:00:10 UTC 2024 x86_64 GNU/Linux
$ exit
logout

❯ podman run -it ubuntu:latest
root@f08dd3460b57:/# uname -a
Linux f08dd3460b57 5.13.0-52-generic #59-Ubuntu SMP Wed Jun 15 20:20:55 UTC 2022 aarch64 aarch64 aarch64 GNU/Linux
root@f08dd3460b57:/#
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expected an x86 container

### podman info output

```yaml

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Attempt to specify arch failed

```
❯ podman run --arch=amd64 -it ubuntu:latest
Resolved "ubuntu" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 49b384cc7b4a done
Copying config bf3dc08bfe done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
exec /bin/bash: exec format error
```

Platform flag doesn't work as well

```
❯ podman run --platform linux/amd64 -it ubuntu:latest
exec /bin/bash: exec format error
```

This looks like you are connected to an arm64 machine.

What happens when  you execute `podman machine ssh` do you end up in the lima machine?  If yes what version of runc is showing within the machine?

Podman info is showing?

    package: runc_1.1.0-0ubuntu1~21.10.1_arm64

I thought that it might be caused by a conflict between simultaneously running `podman desktop` and `lima`. So I stopped `podman desktop` and other lima based VMs and repeated the experiment.

```
❯ limactl list
NAME              STATUS     SSH                VMTYPE    ARCH       CPUS    MEMORY    DISK      DIR
box               Stopped    127.0.0.1:0        vz        aarch64    4       4GiB      100GiB    ~/.lima/box
default           Stopped    127.0.0.1:0        qemu      aarch64    4       4GiB      100GiB    ~/.lima/default
docker            Stopped    127.0.0.1:0        qemu      x86_64     4       4GiB      100GiB    ~/.lima/docker
podman-rootful    Running    127.0.0.1:54252    qemu      x86_64     4       4GiB      100GiB    ~/.lima/podman-rootful
podman-testing    Stopped    127.0.0.1:0        qemu      aarch64    4       4GiB      100GiB    ~/.lima/podman-testing
x86_64            Stopped    127.0.0.1:0        qemu      x86_64     4       4GiB      100GiB    ~/.lima/x86_64
```

The podman-rootful is x86_64 (in this experiment I am using qemu (therefore no bindfmt)). 

```
❯ podman system connection add lima-podman-rootful "unix:///Users/iilyak/.lima/podman-rootful/sock/podman.sock"

❯ podman system connection default lima-podman-rootful
```

the test itself

```
❯ podman run --platform=linux/arm64 ubuntu:latest uname -a
Linux b3d93a2dfceb 6.8.5-301.fc40.x86_64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu Apr 11 20:00:10 UTC 2024 aarch64 aarch64 aarch64 GNU/Linux

❯ podman run --platform=linux/amd64 ubuntu:latest uname -a
Resolved "ubuntu" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Linux 72aa08a0ecab 6.8.5-301.fc40.x86_64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu Apr 11 20:00:10 UTC 2024 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

By the way I am quite surprised why both works if bindfmt is not in play.

Trying to choose default arch

```
❯ podman run -it ubuntu:latest uname -a
Linux 42e8d805eb0a 6.8.5-301.fc40.x86_64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu Apr 11 20:00:10 UTC 2024 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

Let's see what would be choosen If I change the host shell to x86 arch 

```
❯ arch -x86_64 fish
Welcome to fish, the friendly interactive shell

❯ podman run -it ubuntu:latest uname -a
Linux 1920b092a777 6.8.5-301.fc40.x86_64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu Apr 11 20:00:10 UTC 2024 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

I think it is all works as expected. Probably user error.
